questions.text: Robots are becoming easier to program and more flexible to use, which enables a single factory to manufacture many different products.
options: Giderek daha kolay programlanabilir ve daha esnek şekilde kullanılabilir robotların ortaya çıkması sayesinde fabrikalar birçok farklı ürünü imal edebilir.
Fabrikaların birçok ürünü imal edebilmesinin arkasında, giderek daha kolay programlanabilir ve daha esnek şekilde kullanılabilir hale gelen robotlar vardır.
Bir fabrikanın birçok farklı ürünü imal edebilmesi, ancak robotların daha kolay programlanabilir ve daha esnek şekilde kullanılabilir olmasıyla mümkündür.
Robotlar giderek daha kolay programlanabilir ve daha esnek şekilde kullanılabilir bir hâle gelmekte, bu da tek bir fabrikanın birçok farklı ürünü imal etmesine olanak sağlamaktadır.
Giderek daha kolay programlanabilir ve daha esnek şekilde kullanılabilir robotların sayısının artmasıyla birlikte, tek bir fabrika birçok farklı ürünü imal edebilir hale gelecektir.
questions.correct_answer: Robotlar giderek daha kolay programlanabilir ve daha esnek şekilde kullanılabilir bir hâle gelmekte, bu da tek bir fabrikanın birçok farklı ürünü imal etmesine olanak sağlamaktadır.

Yaşamımızın ilk on-iki yılında beynimizde ve davranışlarımızda meydana gelen hızlı değişimler birbirinden ayırt edilemeyecek kadar benzerlik gösterirler.
Yaşamımızın ilk on-iki yılında beynimizde ve davranışlarımızda meydana gelen hızlı değişimler birbirleriyle uyumlu şekilde gelişir.
Yaşamın ilk yıllarında meydana gelen davranışsal değişimler ile bu süreç içinde beyin yapısında oluşan bazı değişimler arasında benzerlikler mevcuttur.
Yaşamın ilk on-iki yılında hızlı bir şekilde meydana gelen davranışsal değişimler, bu süreçte beyin yapısında oluşan değişimlere büyük ölçüde uyum gösterir.
questions.correct_answer: Yaşamın ilk on-iki yılında hızlı bir şekilde meydana gelen davranışsal değişimler, bu zaman zarfında beyin yapısında oluşan değişimlerle uyumludur.

questions.text: 8 KDİL Today, only billionaires can afford a private flight into space, but by 2150 the cost of such a flight will probably be no more than that of a first class airline ticket.

Uzaya özel uçakla gitmek sadece milyarderlere özgü bir ayrıcalıktır, ancak böyle bir uçuşun maliyeti 2150’ye kadar birinci sınıf bir uçak biletiyle aynı olacaktır.
Geçmişte uzaya özel uçakla gitmeye yalnızca milyarderlerin gücü yetiyordu, ancak böyle bir uçuşun maliyeti 2150’ye kadar bir uçak bileti ücretine denk gelecek şekilde ucuzlayacaktır.
questions.correct_answer: Günümüzde uzaya özel uçuş yapmaya sadece milyarderlerin gücü yetmektedir, ancak böyle bir uçuşun maliyeti 2150’ye kadar muhtemelen birinci sınıf bir uçak biletinden daha fazla olmayacaktır.

Kuantum bilgisayarlar, geleneksel bilgisayarlardan daha güçlü olabilirler, ancak bazı uygulamaların bir kuantum bilgisayarın tek başına sağlayabileceğinden daha fazla işlem gücü gerektireceği unutulmamalıdır.

questions.correct_answer: Kuantum bilgisayarlar, geleneksel bilgisayarlardan daha güçlü olabilirler, ancak bazı uygulamalar bir kuantum bilgisayarın tek başına sağlayabileceğinden daha fazla işlem gücü gerektirecektir.

questions.text: 9 KDİL 48 - 53. sorularda, verilen Türkçe cümleye anlamca en yakın İngilizce cümleyi bulunuz. Evrendeki diğer temel güçlerle karşılaştırıldığında oldukça zayıf olmasına rağmen, yerçekimi evrendeki en devasa cisimlerin hareketlerinin arkasındaki itici güçtür.
options: Compared to the other fundamental forces in the universe, gravity is weak, yet it is considered to be the driving force behind the motions of many objects in the universe.
When compared to the other vital forces in the universe, gravity is the weakest, but it is still the driving force behind the motions of the most massive objects in the universe.
Although it is quite weak compared to the other fundamental forces in the universe, gravity is the driving force behind the motions of the most massive objects in the universe.
Gravity is the basic driving force behind the motions of many massive objects in the universe despite the fact that it is the weakest force in the universe.
Gravity, admittedly weaker than the other basic forces in the universe, is the driving force behind the motions of several massive objects in the universe.
questions.correct_answer: Although it is quite weak compared to the other fundamental forces in the universe, gravity is the driving force behind the motions of the most massive objects in the universe.

questions.text: Gözlem verileri birçok galaksinin merkezinde kara deliklerin bulunabileceğini gösteriyor.
options: Data supported by observations show that there are black holes at the centre of most galaxies, if not all.
Observational data indicate that black holes may exist at the centre of many galaxies.
Data based on recent observations suggest that many galaxies are likely to have black holes at their centre.
There are most probably black holes at the centre of many galaxies as proved by recent observational data
According to observational data, it is quite evident that there are black holes at the centre of most galaxies, if not all.
questions.correct_answer: Observational data indicate that black holes may exist at the centre of many galaxies.

questions.text: Derhâl müdahale edilmediği takdirde iklim değişikliğinin küresel çapta felaketlere ve binlerce ölüme neden olabileceği tahmin edilmektedir.
options: It is estimated that climate change, if not addressed urgently, may cause disasters and thousands of deaths globally.
It is calculated that climate change will cause disasters and thousands of deaths if it is not addressed in the near future.
Climate change is thought to cause major disasters and thousands of deaths even after the recent measures.
Climate change will likely cause some major disasters and thousands of deaths globally if it is not tackled properly.
Climate change, if not handled appropriately, is likely to cause major disasters and thousands of deaths globally.
questions.correct_answer: It is estimated that climate change, if not addressed urgently, may cause disasters and thousands of deaths globally.

questions.text: Dünyanın başka hiçbir yerinde bulunmayan sayısız türün evi olarak bilinen tropik yağmur ormanları, daha önce hiç görülmemiş bir hızda yok ediliyor.
options: Tropical rainforests are the home to numerous species found nowhere else in the world, but they are being destroyed rapidly.
Tropical rainforests, which are the home of numerous species existing nowhere else in the world, are being saved at an unprecedented rate.
As tropical rainforests are being destroyed at an alarming rate, numerous species found nowhere else in the world are becoming extinct at an unprecedented rate.
Known to be the home of numerous species found nowhere else in the world, tropical rainforests are destroyed at an unprecedented rate.
Although it is known to be the home of numerous species rarely found in the world, tropical rainforests are destroyed at an alarming rate.
questions.correct_answer: Known to be the home of numerous species found nowhere else in the world, tropical rainforests are destroyed at an unprecedented rate.

questions.text: 0 KDİL Bilim insanları, yetersiz petrol ve gaz kaynaklarına sahip olan ülkelerin, yenilenebilir enerjiye geçmesini öneriyor.
options: Due to limited oil and gas resources in the country, scientists strongly recommend a shift to renewable energy.
Scientists recommend that countries with insufficient oil and gas resources should shift to renewable energy.
The restricted oil and gas resources in the country forced scientists to propose a rapid shift to renewable energy.
Considering how little oil and gas resources countries have, scientists recommend a shift to renewable energy.
Since the country has limited oil and gas resources, scientists propose to use more renewable energy.
questions.correct_answer: Scientists recommend that countries with insufficient oil and gas resources should shift to renewable energy.

questions.text: Bazı tasarımcılar için üretim sürecinde yapıtları üzerinde  belli bir ölçüde denetime sahip olmaları önemlidir.
options: Retaining a degree of control over their work in the production stage is considered vital by designers.
According to designers, retaining some degree of control over the production process is really important.
For designers who produce certain works, it is necessary to retain a degree of control over their work.
It is important for some designers to retain a degree of control over their work in the production process.
Some designers believe that retaining control over their work in the production process is vital.
questions.correct_answer: It is important for some designers to retain a degree of control over their work in the production process.

questions.text: bütünlüğünü sağlamak için boş bırakılan yerlere  getirilebilecek cümleyi bulunuz. There are many ways to define ‘time’ at a particular location. Here, on Earth, our usual system is defined by the motion of the Sun in the sky. This means that the local time depends on where you are on Earth and we get around this complication by having many different time zones. Now, we could also define a similar time system based on the motion of the Sun as seen from the Moon. Such a system exists but it is not much more than an interesting exercise in physics. -------. This is called Universal Time, and it is a modern form of Greenwich Mean Time. It is the same everywhere in the Universe.
options: - 59. sorularda, parçada anlam bütünlüğünü sağlamak için boş bırakılan yerlere  getirilebilecek cümleyi bulunuz. There are many ways to define ‘time’ at a particular location. Here, on Earth, our usual system is defined by the motion of the Sun in the sky. This means that the local time depends on where you are on Earth and we get around this complication by having many different time zones. Now, we could also define a similar time system based on the motion of the Sun as seen from the Moon. Such a system exists but it is not much more than an interesting exercise in physics. -------. This is called Universal Time, and it is a modern form of Greenwich Mean Time. It is the same everywhere in the Universe. 54. What is more useful, however, is a definition of time that does not vary with location
It is difficult to keep track of time at work due to the workload
There have been several unsuccessful attempts to define a brand-new computing system
So, the time on the Moon can never be the same as the time on Earth
Therefore, it will never be possible to calculate exactly what time it is on the Moon
questions.correct_answer: - 59. sorularda, parçada anlam bütünlüğünü sağlamak için boş bırakılan yerlere  getirilebilecek cümleyi bulunuz. There are many ways to define ‘time’ at a particular location. Here, on Earth, our usual system is defined by the motion of the Sun in the sky. This means that the local time depends on where you are on Earth and we get around this complication by having many different time zones. Now, we could also define a similar time system based on the motion of the Sun as seen from the Moon. Such a system exists but it is not much more than an interesting exercise in physics. -------. This is called Universal Time, and it is a modern form of Greenwich Mean Time. It is the same everywhere in the Universe. 54. What is more useful, however, is a definition of time that does not vary with location

questions.text: High-altitude landscapes are some of the most inhospitable places on Earth. They are cold, dry, and oxygen-poor, which causes sleeplessness, fatigue, coughing, confusion, and a rapid pulse. They were the last places humans settled, yet people did it and survived. -------. From the Himalayas to the Andes to the Ethiopian Plateau, people have evolved in ways that allow them to live at high altitude.
options: The Himalayas and the Andes attract many climbers looking for adventure
This was all about our failure to cope with such an extreme phenomenon
Living at such heights causes hypoxia, a condition where tissues cannot get enough oxygen
It is easier to find well-protected remains of ancient empires than ever before due to technology
The only logical explanation for this is our ability to adapt, especially to extreme environments
questions.correct_answer: The only logical explanation for this is our ability to adapt, especially to extreme environments

questions.text: 1 KDİL Ceres, an unassuming-looking object made of ice and rock, lies between Mars and Jupiter in the expanse of the Asteroid Belt. It was first spotted by Sicilian astronomer Giuseppe Piazzi on 1 January 1801. This 965-kilometre diameter ball could just have been considered a dead, grey rock in space. -------. It has been discovered that it was – and possibly still is – an active world that may have had cryovolcanoes of the kind that has pretty much disappeared from Earth.
options: There are also some other mountains on Ceres such as Liberalia and Yamor
Just one solitary peak made of ice, mud and salts, called Ahuna Mons
But as NASA’s Dawn spacecraft has recently uncovered, Ceres has been hiding a strange secret
These planets are not the only bodies discovered in our Solar system
That is, if it erupts icy materials like water ice, or methane instead of lava
questions.correct_answer: But as NASA’s Dawn spacecraft has recently uncovered, Ceres has been hiding a strange secret

questions.text: As honeybees develop, they undergo metamorphosis. -------. At the right moment, the protective wax caps put in place by worker bees are removed, revealing the pupae just days before they are ready to emerge as fully grown adults. Bees in this stage are pale, as their outer shells are not yet hardened. When they emerge as adults, their shells can still take a few hours to fully harden. This means that very young bees are more or less unable to sting.
options: The shell of a new born bee is not as hard as it is supposed to be
The queen bee is not involved in the process of wax cell production
It is a great idea to mark honeybees if you want to follow them
Their lifecycle consists of four stages: egg, larva, pupa, and adult
Young bees, compared to fully grown adults, are less dangerous to us
questions.correct_answer: Their lifecycle consists of four stages: egg, larva, pupa, and adult

questions.text: Late in the twentieth century, evidence of global warming mounted as ice sheets in the Arctic and Antarctic began melting rapidly. Carbon dioxide levels climbed, and the protective ozone layer shrank. Although Earth had experienced cycles of abnormal warmth and cold even before humans appeared, most scientists and some political leaders feared that human activity was seriously disrupting the world’s climate. They urged energy conservation and alternatives to carbon-rich oil and coal, such as solar and wind power, hydrogen, and synthetic fuels. -------.
options: Like many developed countries, the United States, proportionally the world’s largest energy user, supports cost-efficient projects
As a result, 140 nations approved an environmental agreement in 2005, known as the Kyoto Protocols, in order to limit destructive emissions
It has been long known that electric utilities using fossil fuels produce greater air pollution than nuclear power plants
When the cold war was over, many nations started scientific space exploration to discover habitable planets
However, one of the major challenges facing developing countries today is to find sufficient oil resources
questions.correct_answer: As a result, 140 nations approved an environmental agreement in 2005, known as the Kyoto Protocols, in order to limit destructive emissions

questions.text: There are objects that are classified neither as stars nor as planets, called ‘brown dwarfs’. -------. Brown dwarfs accumulate matter in the same way as stars, but fail to attain enough mass to ignite hydrogen fusion. Stars can fuse hydrogen into helium, which can only occur above a certain temperature and pressure. Thus, objects above this threshold are stars. Brown dwarfs initially produce heat by fusing an isotope of hydrogen called deuterium into helium-3, which occurs at lower temperatures and lower masses.
options: NASA is looking for more information about the planets’ atmosphere
Thus, more precise measurements will no longer be needed
Such information will also explain the processes that shaped the young Milky Way
Researchers originally thought that the system had three planets
This term was first coined by Jill Tarter of the SETI Institute
questions.correct_answer: This term was first coined by Jill Tarter of the SETI Institute

questions.text: 2 KDİL 60 - 65. sorularda, cümleler sırasıyla okunduğunda parçanın anlam bütünlüğünü bozan cümleyi bulunuz. (I) Such an assumption has not yet been tested rigorously for most animal species. (II) Vertebrates are often marked to facilitate the identification of free-ranging individual animals or groups for studies of behaviour, population biology, and physiology. (III) Marked animals provide data for home range use, resource selection, social behaviour, and population estimation. (IV) Markers can be classified into three general categories: mutilations, tags and bands, and radio transmitters. (V) The appropriate marking technique for a study depends on several considerations, including study objectives, target species, marker cost, marker efficacy, and marker effects on the animals.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: I

questions.text: (I) An environmentalist, on the other hand, is someone who actively works to preserve the environment from destruction or pollution. (II) Environment includes everything that affects an organism during its lifetime. (III) In turn, all organisms, including people, affect many components in their environment. (IV) From a human point of view, environmental issues involve concerns about science, nature, health, employment, profit, law, politics, ethics, fine arts, and economics. (V) Therefore, environmental science is by its nature a multidisciplinary field.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: I

questions.text: (I) An alternative career opportunity for engineers has been offered by Frederick Taylor. (II) He has proposed that engineers should take over the role of managers in the governance of companies and that of politicians in the governance of society. (III) This proposal would lead to the establishment of a technocracy, that is, government by experts. (IV) The role of engineers would, thus, be that of technocrats who, on the basis of technological insight, do what they consider best for a company or for society. (V) For instance, being an engineer requires great responsibility for a number of reasons.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: V

questions.text: 3 KDİL (I) Our food and materials come to us via a system that spans the world and whose consequences are mostly concealed from us. (II) On average, food is said to travel more than 1,300 miles from where it was grown or produced to where it is eaten. (III) In such a system, there is no conceivable way of knowing the human or ecological consequences of eating. (IV) The average citizen of the United States now uses some 186,000 calories of energy each day. (V) Nor can we know the full cost of virtually food that we purchase or discard.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: IV

questions.text: (I) ENIAC, the first electronic computer, was completed in 1945 at the University of Pennsylvania under a military contract. (II) Engineer J. Presper Eckert and physicist John W. Mauchly’s enormous device was powered by 18,000 vacuum tubes and performed 5,000 calculations per second. (III) While computers allegedly reduce paper documents, new copying and printing technologies only increase the flood. (IV) Hungarian refugee John von Neumann soon after developed what became the basic architecture of computer systems. (V) The invention of transistors by lab scientists at Bell Laboratories in 1948 eventually eliminated clumsy vacuum tubes and paved the way for microchips.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: III

questions.text: (I) Dinosaurs were ‘born’ officially in 1842. (II) They were all land-living animals, which poses particular problems. (III) They owe their birth to the work of the British anatomist Richard Owen, whose work had concentrated upon the unique nature of some extinct fossil reptiles. (IV) At the time of Owen’s review, he was working on a surprisingly large collection of fossil bones and teeth that had been discovered up to that time and were scattered around the British Isles. (V) Although the birth of dinosaurs was relatively inauspicious, they were soon to become the centre of worldwide attention.
options: I
II
III
IV
V
questions.correct_answer: II

questions.text: 4 KDİL 66 - 68. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Farmers are under pressure to reduce chemical pesticides and their ability to do so will be aided by the news that the U.S. Department of Agriculture is about to start trialling a device that can kill insects with a laser. Developed by an American company, the ‘Photonic Fence’ is not really a fence at all, but a small box containing lasers, cameras and an Artificial Intelligence (AI) computer system. The cameras scan the air around the device for 100 metres, and the AI system measures the shape, speed and acceleration frequency of any bugs detected to establish which are potentially harmful. Any insects identified as a threat can then be zapped by the lasers, with a ‘kill rate’ of up to 20 insects per second. By deploying several such devices, farmers could effectively create a virtual fence around their crops that kills harmful pests but leaves bees and other beneficial or harmless insects untouched. The U.S. trials will begin soon. If the device is proven to work, then the company hopes to bring a commercial product to market, though that will still be some years away. We understand from the text that the ‘Photonic Fence’ -------.
options: has not been mass produced yet
removes all types of insects on farms
may not be effective since it only kills a few insects
is a box which looks like a real fence
will certainly be a waste of money due to its high cost
questions.correct_answer: has not been mass produced yet

questions.text: It is clear from the text that farmers ------- .
options: are against the use of the Photonic Fence in farming
are not allowed to use any chemical pesticides in farming
will continue using pesticide as it is cheaper
are expected to decrease the amounts of chemical pesticides they use
will not be able to afford the Photonic Fence at the moment
questions.correct_answer: are expected to decrease the amounts of chemical pesticides they use

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: a new device which aims to destroy harmful insects via laser technology
the reasons why most American farmers have recently stopped using pesticides
how a company devised the ‘Photonic Fence’ to save harmful pests
the technical challenges of designing the ‘Photonic Fence’ experienced by scientists
methods of distinguishing harmful pests from beneficial ones in farming
questions.correct_answer: a new device which aims to destroy harmful insects via laser technology

questions.text: 5 KDİL 69 - 71. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Wernher von Braun is famous for being the creator of the space program that made it possible to put the first person on the moon on July 20, 1969. He grew up in Germany. From an early age he was fascinated by rocket technology and was involved in developing rockets for the German army in the 1930s. In 1937, he joined Hitler’s National Socialist Party, about which he later stated: “My refusal to join the party would have meant that I would have had to abandon the work of my life. Therefore, I decided to join”. In 1943, it was claimed that he sabotaged the V2 rocket program, and was arrested by the Nazis. Later, however, he was released. In 1945, he realized that the Germans were going to lose the war, and arranged for his team to be handed over to the Americans. In the U.S., Von Braun originally worked on the development of rockets for military purposes. His dream came true by playing a key role in the space travel program. Von Braun’s big dream did therefore ultimately come true. According to the text, Von Braun joined Hitler’s party -------.
options: to be able to get enough information about the rocket programs of other nations
in order to continue his career in rocket technology
because his refusal would lead to his family’s arrest
since he was the only person with profound knowledge of the moon
to get permission from Hitler to work for both Germany and the U.S.
questions.correct_answer: in order to continue his career in rocket technology

questions.text: It is understood from the text that Von Braun -------.
options: had no interest in science when he was a little child
joined Hitler’s party to improve his military skills
spent most of his childhood in America
sabotaged the V2 rocket program to help the U.S. in the war
decided to work for the Americans together with his team
questions.correct_answer: decided to work for the Americans together with his team

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: scientists who played an active role in military operations
the role of science and scientists in the Second World War
the determination of Von Braun to fulfil his life-long dream
Von Braun’s teenage enthusiasm for rocket design
how the U.S. became victorious in the Second World War
questions.correct_answer: the determination of Von Braun to fulfil his life-long dream

questions.text: 6 KDİL 72 - 74. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. ‘Heritability’ is a measure of the relative importance of genes in determining variation in qualitative traits among individuals. However, the calculated heritability value is unique to the population in which it was measured and to the environment of that population. The specificity of heritability means that we should be very cautious when using heritability to measure the general importance of genes to the development of a trait. A famous misapplication of heritability comes from the book The Bell Curve, by Charles Herrnstein and Richard Murray, published in 1994. In this book, the authors report that IQ scores differ among subpopulations in the United States. Among white Americans, IQ averages are around 100 while among African-American populations, IQ averages are nearly 15 points lower. Using a conservative estimate of the heritability of intelligence, they argued that the IQ differences between whites and blacks are primarily due to a genetic difference in intelligence between these groups. However, on closer look, we can see that Herrnstein and Murray’s conclusion is flawed. These differences could be entirely due to environment. Given the history and current social and economic status of African-Americans in the United States, it is certainly possible that their environment is less enriched than the average environment experienced by a white individual. According to the text, Herrnstein and Murray’s research -------.
options: is flawless in terms of methods applied
is the best work on IQ up to date
is questionable due to a mistake in application of heritability
was published in the first half of the twentieth century
deals with environmental problems
questions.correct_answer: is questionable due to a mistake in application of heritability

questions.text: It can be clearly understood from the text that -------.
options: environment is the only variable affecting the intelligence level
there is no simple causal relationship between IQ and race
the heritability value of IQ is constant for all populations and cultures
white Americans scored lower than African-Americans in the IQ test
Herrnstein and Murray have rejected using IQ scores in their research
questions.correct_answer: there is no simple causal relationship between IQ and race

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: the high living standards of African-Americans
the different editions of a book on IQ scores titled The Bell Curve
IQ levels of African-Americans living in the United States
a comparative study of IQ scores between two groups in Africa
a well-known misapplication of heritability in IQ testing
questions.correct_answer: a well-known misapplication of heritability in IQ testing

questions.text: 7 KDİL 75 - 77. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. In addition to temperature, other factors such as the availability of water, prevalence of high winds, and the duration and intensity of sunlight also limit the geographical areas in which various crops can be grown. However, as far as getting the best crop is concerned, even when fruit trees have bloomed satisfactorily, temperature can be a determinant of whether a good crop will be harvested. Most deciduous fruits need pollination, which is normally done by honeybees. If the temperature is not right, the bees just quit flying, and that can mean a very poor crop indeed. Even if the bees fly and pollen is spread, the pollen must germinate and the pollen tube grow down to the ovule, a process that can be severely restricted by unseasonably low temperatures. And even when pollination has been successful, the growth of individual grape berries (botanically, grapes are berries) can be restricted by both too high and too low temperatures. It is clear from the text that -------.
options: the success of the pollination process depends solely on the quality of the plant
inadequate sunlight can be disregarded when trees have bloomed satisfactorily
crops may not fully develop unless temperatures are suitable
the development of deciduous fruits is induced by pollination without bees
new drug tests must be conducted on certain plants
questions.correct_answer: crops may not fully develop unless temperatures are suitable

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the text as a factor that affects crop growth?
options: Fluctuations in temperature
The taste of fruits
Existence of sufficient water
Pollination agents like honeybees
Successful pollen germination
questions.correct_answer: The taste of fruits

questions.text: The text is mainly about -------.
options: the significant role of temperature in growing good crops
the role of temperature on the post-harvest process
adaptation of pollination in other fields of study
the difficulty in detecting honey bees
factors behind the crop variety in some regions
questions.correct_answer: the significant role of temperature in growing good crops

questions.text: 8 KDİL 78 - 80. soruları aşağıda verilen parçaya göre cevaplayınız. Fusion energy almost sounds too good to be true because of zero greenhouse gas emissions, no long-lived radioactive waste, and nearly unlimited fuel supply. Yet, fusion power designs are not cheap enough to outperform systems that use fossil fuels such as coal and natural gas. However, University of Washington engineers hope to change that. They have designed a concept for a fusion reactor that, when scaled up to the size of a large electrical power plant, would rival costs for a new coal-fired plant with similar electrical output. The design builds on existing technology and creates a magnetic field within a closed space to hold plasma in place long enough for fusion to occur, allowing the hot plasma to react and burn. The reactor itself would be largely self-sustaining, meaning it would continuously heat the plasma to maintain thermonuclear conditions. Heat generated from the reactor would heat up a coolant that is used to spin a turbine and generate electricity, similar to how a typical power plant works. It is clear from the text that-------.
options: engineers have succeeded in making current energy systems more environmentally friendly
a fusion reactor can produce much more power than present plants
for the time being, the cost of fusion energy design is too high to be used widely
the fusion power plant design is based entirely on new technology
a cheaper way to produce energy from fossil fuels is under way
questions.correct_answer: for the time being, the cost of fusion energy design is too high to be used widely

questions.text: It is clear from the text that -------.
options: electricity production in a fusion reactor is more time-consuming than other power plants
coal-fired plants will produce less energy if fusion plants are operational
the number of coal-fired power plants is declining due to fusion reactors in use
when in use, the fusion power plant will not release any harmful gases
fusion reactors and fossil-fuelled power plants can both be turned into self-sustaining energy sources
questions.correct_answer: when in use, the fusion power plant will not release any harmful gases

questions.text: The text mainly deals with -------.
options: various cost-efficient means of producing energy
existing fusion power plants and their benefits
research on future plants run by fossil fuels
the cost of fusion power plants and coal-fired plants
a prospective fusion reactor design with great potential
questions.correct_answer: a prospective fusion reactor design with great potential
